calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-25-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA). Paul Malone for Nadine Paul, et al heirs.

Mr. Ferdon addressed the fees and stated that SFPA steps in when the Court asks or in cases where the heirs can act but don't act. In this case, he stated that there were heirs but they chose not to act, that the property contained toxic waste, that there had been a tenant in the property, that the family was involved but they chose not to act, and that there were continuances because the family requested special notice. In addition, that there ended up being 35 relatives and none would act, that they were able to sell the property even though it was toxic, and that they had the property sold by the next November. In closing, Mr. Ferdon stated that the relatives were calling the SFPA on a constant basis, that he invited them to take over, and that the amount the SFPA is requesting is a modest amount. Mr. Malone stated that there was no objection and that his clients want a resolution/distribution. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the written order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy McConico. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (53 Sanchez Street, #55, San Francisco, Ca 94114)
Off Calendar Nov-28-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonahtan Ferdon for Public Administrator.

The current Buyers were not present in Court. James Wong (Broker) represented to the Court that the current Buyers were not interested in placing an over bid. The sale was confirmed at $1,540,000.00 to Howard Yellen based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed October 15, 2012. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to receipt and review of the order). Bond is not required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (53 Sanchez Street, #55, San Francisco, Ca 94114)
Off Calendar Nov-13-2012 Continued To Nov-20-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Guardian. James Wong (Listing Agent). Howard Yellen (Bidder)

Mr. Ferdon stated that he saw the Examiner's notes this morning and that he is unable to respond with regard to the notice issue. The Court raised the issue with regard to the address of the property (53 Sanchez Street, #55). Is it a two unit building? Was the appraisal for the entire property? The amount of the appraisal is half of the bid amount. Mr. Wong stated that he was present when the Probate Referee appraised the property and that the appraisal was for the entire property. Mr. Yellen discribed the poor condition of the property for the low appraisal value. The Court wants a note from the Probate Referee that both units have been appraised and Mr. Ferdon to address the notice issue. The matter was continued to November 20, 2012 at 9:00 a.m.., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-02-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator. Giulio Accornero for Walter Ramsey, Jr. and Terry Wayne Jackson. Paul Malone for Gary Emerson and 25 other heirs.

The Court stated that the waiver of notice signed by Mr. Accornero for his clients is not effective and that they are entitled to receive separate and independent notice. Mr. Accornero presented a proof of service filed on June 28, 2012 and stated that notice was sent to his two clients and two other heirs that he does not represented. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administation (subject to review of the order). The Court further stated that for any later event for this case, it will expect waivers of notice signed by the heirs or consents to giving the right to their attorney.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-06-2012 Continued To Jul-02-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Malone for Gary Emerson. Jonathan Ferdon for Petitioner.

Mr. Ferdon stated that he wrote letters to three Attorneys and twenty seven other Heirs at Law followed up by phone calls. Mr. Malone stated that he was specially appearing for Mr. Accornero and Ms. Boessenecker. Mr. Malone also stated that he served notice of the hearing on all of his Clients and the two Parties represented by Ms. Boessenecker, and that he did not think that Mr. Accornero's Clients (3 or 4) received notice. In addition, Mr. Malone explained urgency with the condition of the property (toxic problems), stated that he did notify the Parties of their right to priority, suggested a three week continuance for Mr. Accornero to provide notice to his Clients and raised the issue of confidentiality with respect to his Clients addresses. Mr. Ferdon stated that he would want special letters with authority to sell the property and that there was one Tenant in the property. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to July 2, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to complete notice. The Court also raised the issue of form of the Proof of Service.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-25-2012 Continued To Jun-06-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to June 6, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Ferdon to communicate to other Parties what the Court is requiring with regard to notice to all of the Heirs and with regard to the Attorneys representing the Heirs filing something in writing by each of their Clients showing that the Attorney may act for them and that they are waiving the right to seek appointment.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Rosada.
